docs.title: laser.generic.ImmunizationCampaign(model, period, coverage, age_lower, age_upper, start=0, end=-1, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER component that applies an immunization campaign over an age band.

On eligible ticks, all agents with age in [age_lower, age_upper) are considered and immunized with probability coverage. Susceptibles become immune (population.susceptibility[idx] = 0). This aligns with the campaign-style immunization component described in the laser-generic docs.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize an ImmunizationCampaign instance.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model object

LASER Model with population, patches, and params. params.nticks must be defined.

required period int

Number of ticks between campaign events. Must be >= 1.

required coverage float

Per-event immunization probability in [0.0, 1.0].

required age_lower int

Inclusive lower bound of target age band (ticks).

required age_upper int

Exclusive upper bound of target age band (ticks). Must be > age_lower.

required start int

First tick (inclusive) to run campaigns. Default 0.

0 end int

Last tick (exclusive) to run campaigns. If -1, defaults to model.params.nticks. Default -1.

-1 verbose bool

Enable verbose logging. Default False.

False

Attributes:

Name Type Description model object

The LASER model instance.

period int

Ticks between campaign events.

coverage float

age_lower int

Inclusive lower age (ticks).

age_upper int

Exclusive upper age (ticks).

First campaign tick (inclusive).

end int

verbose bool

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If inputs are out of range (e.g., period < 1, coverage not in [0, 1], age bounds invalid).

docs.title: laser.generic.ImmunizationCampaign.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Apply the immunization campaign at the given tick, if eligible.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Triggers when

tick >= start and ((tick - start) % period == 0) and tick < end

On each event

  • Agents with age in [age_lower, age_upper) are considered.
  • A Binomial draw with probability coverage selects agents to immunize.
  • Selected agents have susceptibility set to 0 (immune).
  • If present, test arrays on model.patches are updated for validation.

Defaults to 0 if not provided. - importation_end (int, optional): Last tick to introduce infections. Defaults to nticks if not provided.

docs.title: laser.generic.Infect_Random_Agents.__call__(model, tick)

  • The current tick is greater than or equal to start.
  • The tick falls on a multiple of period (relative to start).

This updates both the agent-level infections and, if present, the test arrays in model.patches for validation.

docs.title: laser.generic.Infection(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
LASER component for managing infections in an SIR-style model.

Each agent has an itimer (infectious timer). When itimer > 0, the agent is infectious. On each tick, itimer is decremented. When it reaches zero, the agent transitions to the recovered state (state=3).

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the Infection component.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing: * population with attributes count and method add_scalar_property(name, dtype, default) * patches with method add_vector_property(name, length, dtype) * params.nticks (int): number of simulation ticks verbose : bool, optional If True, enable verbose output. Default is False.

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Adds a scalar property itimer (dtype=uint16, default=0) to the population.
  • Adds a vector property recovered (dtype=uint32) to the patches.
  • Initializes all infection timers to zero.

docs.title: laser.generic.Infection.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Update infection timers and patch-level case/recovery counts.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Decrements itimer for all infectious agents.
  • Agents whose itimer reaches 0 transition to recovered (state=3).
  • Patch-level accounting:
  • patches.cases_test[t+1] is decremented by number of transitions.
  • patches.recovered_test[t+1] is incremented by the same number.

docs.title: laser.generic.Infection.census(model, tick)
docs.text: Aggregate recovered counts into patches at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At tick 0, recovered counts are computed from the population and written to patches.recovered.
  • Recovery condition:
  • With etimers: susceptibility=0, etimer=0, itimer=0
  • Without etimers: susceptibility=0, itimer=0
  • For a single patch, recovered agents are counted globally.
  • For multiple patches, counts are distributed by agent nodeid.
  • In all cases, values are copied into patches.recovered_test and carried forward to tick+1.

docs.title: laser.generic.Infection.on_birth(model, _tick, istart, iend)
docs.text: Reset infection timers for newborn agents.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing the population. _tick : int Current simulation tick (unused). istart : int or ndarray of int64 Start index of newborns (if slice mode), or array of agent indices (if random-access mode). iend : int or None End index of newborns (exclusive). If None, istart is treated as an array of indices.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Newborns are initialized with itimer=0 (not infectious).

docs.title: laser.generic.Infection.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Plot the distribution of infections by age.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional A Matplotlib Figure. If None, a new figure is created.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None This is a generator that currently yields once (None).

docs.title: laser.generic.Model(scenario, parameters, name='generic')
docs.text:
A LASER simulation model for generic disease dynamics.

The Model manages: - Patch-level populations and their attributes. - Agent-level population initialization. - Integration of components (e.g., Births, Infection, Immunization). - Execution of simulation ticks via run(). - Recording of metrics and plotting/visualization utilities.

Typical usage: 

scenario = pd.DataFrame({"population": [1000, 500], "latitude": [...], "longitude": [...]})
params = PropertySet({"nticks": 100, "seed": 123, "verbose": True})
model = Model(scenario, params)
model.components = [Births, Infection, ImmunizationCampaign]
model.run()
model.visualize(pdf=True)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the model with a scenario and simulation parameters.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: scenario : pd.DataFrame Patch-level data. Must include at least: - population: initial population per patch. - latitude: latitude coordinate. - longitude: longitude coordinate. May also include optional columns like geometry. parameters : PropertySet Simulation parameters. Must include: - nticks (int): number of simulation ticks. - seed (int, optional): RNG seed. - verbose (bool, optional): enable verbose logging. name : str, optional Name of the model. Default is "generic".

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Seeds the random number generator.
  • Initializes patches and population.

docs.title: laser.generic.Model.components property writable
docs.text: Retrieve the list of model components currently configured.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list List of component classes used in the model.

docs.title: laser.generic.Model.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text:
Advance patch populations one tick forward.

Copies population counts from tick t to tick t+1. Components such as births or mortality may then update these values.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : Model The current model instance. tick : int Current tick index.

docs.title: laser.generic.Model.plot(fig=None)
docs.text:
Yield three plots for model visualization:

  1. A scatter plot of scenario patches by location and population.
  2. A histogram of day-of-birth values for the initial population. (requires that population.dob exists, e.g. via a Births component).
  3. A pie chart of cumulative update phase timings.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional An existing matplotlib Figure. If None, a new figure is created.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None Each yield produces one plot, so iterating this generator produces three figures sequentially.

docs.title: laser.generic.Model.run()
docs.text:
Execute the model simulation.

For each tick (0..nticks-1): - Run all censuses (recording metrics). - Run all phases (update components). - Record execution times per census/phase.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
After completion

  • Records self.metrics, self.tstart, and self.tfinish.
  • Prints a timing summary if verbose mode is enabled.

docs.title: Attributes Set
docs.text: tstart : datetime Start time of execution. tfinish : datetime End time of execution. metrics : list List of timing metrics (per tick, per phase).

docs.title: laser.generic.Model.visualize(pdf=True)
docs.text: Generate visualizations for all components.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: pdf : bool, optional If True (default), save plots to a PDF file named " .pdf". If False, display plots interactively with plt.show().

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Saves a PDF file when pdf=True.
  • Calls plt.show() when pdf=False.

docs.title: laser.generic.RoutineImmunization(model, period, coverage, age, start=0, end=-1, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER component that updates immunity via routine immunization (RI).

At eligible ticks, agents whose age (in ticks) falls within an RI window centered at age with half-width period // 2 are sampled with probability coverage and made immune (by setting population.susceptibility[idx] = 0).

This component follows the general component style in laser-generic and can be added to Model.components. See package documentation for details on the component pattern.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize a RoutineImmunization instance.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model object

LASER Model with population, patches, and params. params.nticks must be defined.

required period int

Number of ticks between RI events. Must be >= 1.

required coverage float

Per-event immunization probability in [0.0, 1.0].

required age int

Target age (in ticks) around which to immunize.

required start int

First tick (inclusive) to run RI. Default 0.

0 end int

Last tick (exclusive) to run RI. If -1, defaults to model.params.nticks. Default -1.

-1 verbose bool

Enable verbose logging. Default False.

False

Attributes:

Name Type Description model object

The LASER model instance.

period int

Ticks between RI events.

coverage float

Immunization probability at each event.

age int

Target age in ticks.

start int

First RI tick (inclusive).

end int

Last RI tick (exclusive).

verbose bool

Verbosity flag.

Raises:

Type Description ValueError

If period < 1, coverage not in [0, 1], or age < 0.

docs.title: laser.generic.RoutineImmunization.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Apply routine immunization at the given tick, if eligible.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
An event fires when

tick >= start and ((tick - start) % period == 0) and tick < end

On each event

  • Agents with age in [age - period//2, age + period//2) are considered.
  • A Binomial draw with probability coverage selects agents to immunize.
  • Selected agents have susceptibility set to 0 (immune).
  • If present, test arrays on model.patches are updated for validation.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model object

LASER model (unused; provided for signature parity).

required tick int

Current simulation tick.

required

Returns:

Type Description None

None

docs.title: laser.generic.RoutineImmunization.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Placeholder for RI visualization.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default fig Figure

A Matplotlib Figure to draw into.

None

Returns:

Type Description None

None

docs.title: laser.generic.Susceptibility(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER model component representing the susceptibility of a population.

This component adds a scalar property susceptibility to the population and a vector property to the patches. It tracks which agents are susceptible at any given tick and provides helper functions for updating these values.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the susceptibility component of the model.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object A LASER model instance. Must have population, patches, and params with the following: * population.add_scalar_property(name, dtype, default) * patches.add_vector_property(name, nticks, dtype) * params.nticks (int) - number of simulation ticks. verbose : bool, optional If True, enables verbose output. Default is False.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This method adds a scalar property susceptibility to each agent in the population, initialized to 1 (susceptible).
  • It also adds a vector property susceptibility to each patch, which tracks aggregated susceptible counts over time.

docs.title: laser.generic.Susceptibility.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Make the component callable. Required for LASER component interface.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object The LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: This component does not perform per-tick updates. The method exists only to satisfy the LASER component interface.

docs.title: laser.generic.Susceptibility.census(model, tick)
docs.text: Aggregate susceptible counts into patches at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At tick 0, this computes susceptible counts from the population and writes them into patches.susceptibility.
  • For a single patch, counts are summed globally.
  • For multiple patches, counts are distributed by agent nodeid.
  • In all cases, values are copied into patches.susceptibility_test for validation/debugging.

docs.title: laser.generic.Susceptibility.on_birth(model, _tick, istart, iend)
docs.text: Handle the birth event by setting susceptibility of newborns.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing the population. _tick : int Current simulation tick (unused). istart : int or ndarray of int64 Start index of newborns (if slice mode), or array of agent indices (if random-access mode). iend : int or None End index of newborns (exclusive). If None, istart is treated as an index array for random-access updates.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Newborns are set to susceptible (1).
  • Uses either slice-based or random-access update functions.

docs.title: laser.generic.Susceptibility.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Placeholder for plotting susceptibility distribution by age.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional A Matplotlib Figure to draw into. If None, a new figure would be created in a full implementation.

docs.title: laser.generic.Transmission(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER-generic component that models inter-agent disease transmission within and between patches.

This component calculates the force of infection at each time step using: - Agent state (infectious status) - Node-level infectious prevalence - Network-based movement between patches - Time-varying transmission modifiers (e.g., seasonality or biweekly scaling)

It supports multiple transmission dynamics depending on the structure of the population (e.g., SI, SEIR).

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Initialize the Transmission component and register per-patch properties.

Adds the following properties: - cases: Total cumulative infections per patch per time step - forces: Force of infection for each patch (float32) - incidence: New infections per patch per time step - doi: Date of infection for each individual (set on infection)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

A LASER Model instance, which must expose .patches, .population, and .params.

required verbose bool

If True, prints debug info during initialization (currently unused).

False

docs.title: laser.generic.Transmission.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text:
Compute and apply transmission dynamics for the current tick.

This function: - Computes node-level contagion (infectious density) - Modifies contagion using a network matrix if present - Applies time-varying scalars (seasonality, biweekly modifiers) to beta - Calculates force of infection per patch - Samples new infections using Numba-accelerated kernels depending on model structure

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Notes

The appropriate infection update kernel is selected based on the presence of etimer or itimer.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

The LASER model instance.

required tick int

Current simulation tick.

required

docs.title: laser.generic.Transmission.census(model, tick)
docs.text:
Snapshot infectious counts into cases_test at tick t and propagate to tick t+1.

This method: - Initializes cases_test[t] using individual infection status - Copies cases_test[t] into cases_test[t+1] to maintain continuity

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

The LASER model instance.

required tick int

Current simulation tick.

required

docs.text:
Initialize doi (date of infection) for newborns.

This birth handler sets the date-of-infection field to zero for all new individuals.

docs.title: Glossary

Name Type Description Default model Any

The LASER model.

required _tick int

The current tick (unused).

required istart int

Start index of new agents.

required iend int

End index of new agents (exclusive). If None, assumes single agent at istart.

required

Generate a 2x2 subplot figure showing cases and incidence over time.

For the two most populous patches, this creates: - Line plot of cases over time - Line plot of incidence over time

docs.title: laser.generic.births
docs.text:
This module defines the Births component, which is responsible for simulating births in a population model.

Example:

model = YourModelClass()
births = Births(model)
births(model, tick)
births.plot()

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births(model, verbose=False)
docs.text: A component to handle the birth events in a model. Requires a model with a population attribute that has a dob attribute. It calculates the number of births based on the model's parameters and updates the population accordingly. It also provides methods to plot birth statistics.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Attributes:

Name Type Description model

The model instance containing population and parameters.

verbose bool

Flag to enable verbose output. Default is False.

initializers list

List of initializers to be called on birth events.

metrics DataFrame

DataFrame to holding timing metrics for initializers.

Initialize the Births component.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model object

The model object which must have a population attribute.

required verbose bool

If True, enables verbose output. Defaults to False.

False

Raises:

Type Description AssertionError

If the model does not have a population attribute.

AssertionError

If the model's population does not have a dob attribute.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births.initializers property
docs.text:
Returns the initializers to call on new agent births.

This method retrieves the initializers that are used to set up the initial state or configuration for agents at birth.

Name Type Description list list

A list of initializers - instances of objects with an on_birth method.

Name Type Description DataFrame DataFrame

A Pandas DataFrame of timing metrics for the births initializers.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Adds new agents to each patch based on expected daily births calculated from CBR. Calls each of the registered initializers for the newborns.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

The simulation model containing patches, population, and parameters.

required tick int

The current time step in the simulation.

required

Returns:

Type Description None

None

This method performs the following steps

  1. Calculates the day of the year (doy) and the current year based on the tick.
  2. On the first day of the year, it generates annual births for each patch using a Poisson distribution.
  3. Calculates the number of births for the current day.
  4. Adds the newborns to the population and sets their date of birth.
  5. Assigns node IDs to the newborns.
  6. Calls any additional initializers for the newborns and records the timing of these initializations.
  7. Updates the population counts for the next tick with the new births.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Plots the births in the top 5 most populous patches and a pie chart of birth initializer times.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default fig Figure

A matplotlib Figure object. If None, a new figure will be created. Defaults to None.

None

Yields:

Name Type Description None Any

This function yields twice to allow for intermediate plotting steps.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births_ConstantPop(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
Handles births in models with a constant population size (births == deaths).

This component ensures that the total population remains constant over time by generating daily birth counts that exactly match the expected mortality under a given crude birth rate (CBR). Each birth results in the creation of a new agent with a default susceptible state and a birth timestamp.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Key responsibilities

  • Stochastic generation of new births per patch and time step.
  • Initialization of new agents with appropriate state and properties.
  • Invocation of registered birth-time initializers.
  • Real-time updates to reporting channels (e.g. SEIR counts).

The component assumes that deaths are handled implicitly by replacing expired agents with newly born agents.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Expected parameters

  • cbr: Crude birth rate per 1000 people per year (float).
  • nticks: Number of time steps in the simulation (int).

Initialize the Births_ConstantPop component.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

A Model instance with attributes: - params.cbr: Annual crude birth rate. - params.nticks: Number of time steps. - population: An agent population object. - patches: A patch manager with population counts. - prng: A random number generator supporting .poisson and .exponential.

required verbose bool

Enable detailed logging if True.

False

Raises:

Type Description AssertionError

If the model lacks a population or dob attribute.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births_ConstantPop.initializers property
docs.text:
Returns the initializers to call on new agent births.

This method retrieves the initializers that are used to set up the initial state or configuration for agents at birth.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:

Name Type Description list list

A list of initializers - instances of objects with an on_birth method.

For each patch

  • Determine the number of new agents to birth using a Poisson draw.
  • Assign each agent a birth date and susceptible state.
  • Update SEIR reporting channels to reflect changes.
  • Run all registered on_birth initializers.

The simulation model.

  1. Line plot: Population and birth counts for top 5 most populous patches.
  2. Pie chart: Cumulative time spent in each on_birth initializer.

This method is a generator; call next() twice to display both plots.

docs.title: laser.generic.births.Births_ConstantPop_VariableBirthRate(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
Bases: Births_ConstantPop

Birth process with time-varying crude birth rate (CBR) while maintaining constant population.

This component extends Births_ConstantPop by allowing the birth rate to vary over time, based on a schedule of crude birth rates (CBRs) defined in the model parameters.

Unlike a traditional demographic model, this assumes a constant total population size by balancing births and deaths. The birth rate schedule determines the number of daily births (and therefore deaths) at each timestep.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:

Name Type Description model

The simulation model, which must contain population and patch data.

Name Type Description Default model Any

A Model object with population, patches, and params attributes. params.cbr must be a dictionary with: - "rates": list or array of CBR values (per 1000 people per year). - "timesteps": list or array of corresponding time ticks when rates change.

required verbose bool

If True, print verbose status updates. Defaults to False.

False

Raises:

Type Description AssertionError

If the model lacks a population attribute.

ValueError

If params.cbr is missing or malformed.

docs.title: laser.generic.cli
docs.text:
Module that contains the command line app.

Why does this file exist, and why not put this in main?

You might be tempted to import things from main later, but that will cause problems: the code will get executed twice:

  • When you run python -mlaser.generic python will execute __main__.py as a script. That means there will not be any laser.generic.__main__ in sys.modules.
  • When you import main it will get executed again (as a module) because there"s no laser.generic.__main__ in sys.modules.

Also see (1) from https://click.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/setuptools/

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure
docs.text:
This module defines the Exposure class, which models the transition of individuals from being exposed (infected but not yet infectious) to becoming infectious.

The component manages the etimer (exposed timer) for each agent, updates patch-level exposed/case counts, handles newborns, and provides simple visualization of exposure by age.

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure.Exposure(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
LASER component representing the exposed (latent) state of a population.

Each agent has an etimer (exposed timer). When etimer > 0, the agent is in the exposed state. On each tick, etimer is decremented. When it reaches zero, the agent transitions to the infectious state, and its itimer (infectious timer) is initialized.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the Exposure component.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing: * population with attributes count and method add_scalar_property(name, dtype, default) * patches with method add_vector_property(name, length, dtype) * params.nticks (int): number of simulation ticks verbose : bool, optional If True, enable verbose output. Default is False.

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Adds a scalar property etimer (dtype=uint16, default=0) to the population.
  • Adds a vector property exposed (dtype=uint32) to the patches.
  • Initializes all etimers to zero.

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure.Exposure.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Update exposed timers for the population at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Decrements etimer for all exposed agents.
  • Agents whose etimer reaches 0 transition to infectious:
  • itimer is set to a draw from Normal(inf_mean, inf_sigma), with minimum value 1.
  • state is set to 2 (infectious).
  • Patch-level accounting:
  • patches.exposed_test[t+1] is decremented by the number of transitions.
  • patches.cases_test[t+1] is incremented by the same number.

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure.Exposure.census(model, tick)
docs.text: Aggregate exposed counts into patches at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model with population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At tick 0, exposed counts are computed from the population and written to patches.exposed.
  • For a single patch, exposed agents are counted globally.
  • For multiple patches, exposed agents are distributed according to their nodeid.
  • In all cases, values are copied into patches.exposed_test for validation/debugging and carried forward to tick+1.

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure.Exposure.on_birth(model, _tick, istart, iend)
docs.text: Reset exposure timers for newborn agents.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing the population. _tick : int Current simulation tick (unused). istart : int or ndarray of int64 Start index of newborns (if slice mode), or array of agent indices (if random-access mode). iend : int or None End index of newborns (exclusive). If None, istart is treated as an array of indices.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Newborns are set to not exposed: etimer = 0.

docs.title: laser.generic.exposure.Exposure.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Plot the distribution of exposures by age.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional A Matplotlib Figure. If None, a new figure is created.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None This is a generator that currently yields once (None).

docs.title: laser.generic.immunization
docs.text:
Immunization components for LASER models.

These components introduce immunity into the agent population during a simulation.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Deployment is currently global (all patches). Future extensions may include:
  • targeting by patch or lists of patches,
  • patch-varying coverage,
  • time-varying routine immunization (RI) coverage.
  • The routine immunization window centers on the target age with width ≈ period, i.e., [age - period/2, age + period/2).

docs.title: laser.generic.immunization.ImmunizationCampaign(model, period, coverage, age_lower, age_upper, start=0, end=-1, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER component that applies an immunization campaign over an age band.

On eligible ticks, all agents with age in [age_lower, age_upper) are considered and immunized with probability coverage. Susceptibles become immune (population.susceptibility[idx] = 0). This aligns with the campaign-style immunization component described in the laser-generic docs.

Name Type Description Default model object

LASER Model with population, patches, and params. params.nticks must be defined.

required period int

Number of ticks between campaign events. Must be >= 1.

required coverage float

Per-event immunization probability in [0.0, 1.0].

required age_lower int

Inclusive lower bound of target age band (ticks).

required age_upper int

Exclusive upper bound of target age band (ticks). Must be > age_lower.

required start int

First tick (inclusive) to run campaigns. Default 0.

0 end int

Last tick (exclusive) to run campaigns. If -1, defaults to model.params.nticks. Default -1.

-1 verbose bool

Enable verbose logging. Default False.

False

docs.title: laser.generic.immunization.RoutineImmunization(model, period, coverage, age, start=0, end=-1, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER component that updates immunity via routine immunization (RI).

At eligible ticks, agents whose age (in ticks) falls within an RI window centered at age with half-width period // 2 are sampled with probability coverage and made immune (by setting population.susceptibility[idx] = 0).

This component follows the general component style in laser-generic and can be added to Model.components. See package documentation for details on the component pattern.

docs.title: laser.generic.infection
docs.text: This module defines components for modeling infection dynamics in a LASER population.

docs.title: Classes
docs.text: Infection Handles general infection-to-recovery transitions. Infection_SIS Variant for SIS models, where recovered agents become susceptible again.

docs.title: laser.generic.infection.Infection(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
LASER component for managing infections in an SIR-style model.

Each agent has an itimer (infectious timer). When itimer > 0, the agent is infectious. On each tick, itimer is decremented. When it reaches zero, the agent transitions to the recovered state (state=3).

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the Infection component.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing: * population with attributes count and method add_scalar_property(name, dtype, default) * patches with method add_vector_property(name, length, dtype) * params.nticks (int): number of simulation ticks verbose : bool, optional If True, enable verbose output. Default is False.

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Adds a scalar property itimer (dtype=uint16, default=0) to the population.
  • Adds a vector property recovered (dtype=uint32) to the patches.
  • Initializes all infection timers to zero.

docs.title: laser.generic.infection.Infection.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Update infection timers and patch-level case/recovery counts.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Decrements itimer for all infectious agents.
  • Agents whose itimer reaches 0 transition to recovered (state=3).
  • Patch-level accounting:
  • patches.cases_test[t+1] is decremented by number of transitions.
  • patches.recovered_test[t+1] is incremented by the same number.

docs.title: laser.generic.infection.Infection.census(model, tick)
docs.text: Aggregate recovered counts into patches at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At tick 0, recovered counts are computed from the population and written to patches.recovered.
  • Recovery condition:
  • With etimers: susceptibility=0, etimer=0, itimer=0
  • Without etimers: susceptibility=0, itimer=0
  • For a single patch, recovered agents are counted globally.
  • For multiple patches, counts are distributed by agent nodeid.
  • In all cases, values are copied into patches.recovered_test and carried forward to tick+1.

docs.title: laser.generic.infection.Infection.on_birth(model, _tick, istart, iend)
docs.text: Reset infection timers for newborn agents.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing the population. _tick : int Current simulation tick (unused). istart : int or ndarray of int64 Start index of newborns (if slice mode), or array of agent indices (if random-access mode). iend : int or None End index of newborns (exclusive). If None, istart is treated as an array of indices.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Newborns are initialized with itimer=0 (not infectious).

docs.title: laser.generic.infection.Infection.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Plot the distribution of infections by age.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional A Matplotlib Figure. If None, a new figure is created.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None This is a generator that currently yields once (None).

LASER component for infection in an SIS model.

Each agent has an itimer. When it reaches 0, the agent becomes susceptible again (susceptibility[i] = 1).

docs.title: laser.generic.model
docs.text:
This module defines a Model class for simulating classic "generic" compartmental disease models (SI, SIS, SIR, SEIR, ...). The model supports simple demographics (e.g., births, deaths, aging) and can simulate either a single population patch or multiple patches with an arbitrary connection structure.

Imports: - datetime: For handling date and time operations. - click: For command-line interface utilities. - numpy as np: For numerical operations. - pandas as pd: For data handling and tabular reports. - laser.core.laserframe: Provides the LaserFrame class for structured data arrays. - laser.core.propertyset: Provides the PropertySet class for simulation parameters. - laser.core.random: Provides random number generator seeding utilities. - matplotlib: For plotting results. - tqdm: For progress bar visualization during runs.

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model(scenario, parameters, name='generic')
docs.text:
A LASER simulation model for generic disease dynamics.

The Model manages: - Patch-level populations and their attributes. - Agent-level population initialization. - Integration of components (e.g., Births, Infection, Immunization). - Execution of simulation ticks via run(). - Recording of metrics and plotting/visualization utilities.

Typical usage: 

scenario = pd.DataFrame({"population": [1000, 500], "latitude": [...], "longitude": [...]})
params = PropertySet({"nticks": 100, "seed": 123, "verbose": True})
model = Model(scenario, params)
model.components = [Births, Infection, ImmunizationCampaign]
model.run()
model.visualize(pdf=True)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the model with a scenario and simulation parameters.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: scenario : pd.DataFrame Patch-level data. Must include at least: - population: initial population per patch. - latitude: latitude coordinate. - longitude: longitude coordinate. May also include optional columns like geometry. parameters : PropertySet Simulation parameters. Must include: - nticks (int): number of simulation ticks. - seed (int, optional): RNG seed. - verbose (bool, optional): enable verbose logging. name : str, optional Name of the model. Default is "generic".

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Seeds the random number generator.
  • Initializes patches and population.

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model.components property writable
docs.text: Retrieve the list of model components currently configured.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: list List of component classes used in the model.

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text:
Advance patch populations one tick forward.

Copies population counts from tick t to tick t+1. Components such as births or mortality may then update these values.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : Model The current model instance. tick : int Current tick index.

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model.plot(fig=None)
docs.text:
Yield three plots for model visualization:

  1. A scatter plot of scenario patches by location and population.
  2. A histogram of day-of-birth values for the initial population. (requires that population.dob exists, e.g. via a Births component).
  3. A pie chart of cumulative update phase timings.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional An existing matplotlib Figure. If None, a new figure is created.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None Each yield produces one plot, so iterating this generator produces three figures sequentially.

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model.run()
docs.text:
Execute the model simulation.

For each tick (0..nticks-1): - Run all censuses (recording metrics). - Run all phases (update components). - Record execution times per census/phase.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
After completion

  • Records self.metrics, self.tstart, and self.tfinish.
  • Prints a timing summary if verbose mode is enabled.

docs.title: Attributes Set
docs.text: tstart : datetime Start time of execution. tfinish : datetime End time of execution. metrics : list List of timing metrics (per tick, per phase).

docs.title: laser.generic.model.Model.visualize(pdf=True)
docs.text: Generate visualizations for all components.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: pdf : bool, optional If True (default), save plots to a PDF file named " .pdf". If False, display plots interactively with plt.show().

docs.title: Side Effects
docs.text:
• Saves a PDF file when pdf=True.
  • Calls plt.show() when pdf=False.

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIR
docs.text:
Export required components for an SEIR model.

Agents transition from Susceptible to Exposed upon infection, with an incubation duration. Agents transition from Exposed to Infectious after the incubation period and are infectious for a duration. Agents transition from Infectious to Recovered after the infectious period. Agents remain in the Recovered state indefinitely (no waning immunity).

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIR.Exposed(model, expdurdist, infdurdist, expdurmin=1, infdurmin=1)
docs.text:
Simple Exposed component for an SEIR/SEIRS model - includes incubation period.

Agents transition from Exposed to Infectious when their incubation timer (etimer) expires. Tracks number of agents becoming infectious each tick in model.nodes.symptomatic.

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIR.Infectious(model, infdurdist, infdurmin=1)
docs.text:
Infectious component for an SIR/SEIR model - includes infectious duration, no waning immunity in recovered state.

Agents transition from Infectious to Recovered after the infectious period (itimer). Tracks number of agents recovering each tick in model.nodes.recovered.

docs.text:
Simple Recovered component for an SIR/SEIR model - no waning immunity.

Agents remain in the Recovered state indefinitely (no waning immunity).

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIR.Transmission(model, expdurdist, expdurmin=1)
docs.text:
Transmission component for an SIER/SIERS model with S -> E transition and incubation duration.

Agents transition from Susceptible to Exposed based on force of infection. Sets newly exposed agents' infection timers (etimer) based on expdurdist and expdurmin. Tracks number of new infections each tick in model.nodes.incidence.

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIRS
docs.text:
Export required components for an SEIRS model.

Agents transition from Susceptible to Exposed upon infection, with an incubation duration. Agents transition from Exposed to Infectious after the incubation period and are infectious for a duration. Agents transition from Infectious to Recovered after the infectious period. Agents transition from Recovered back to Susceptible upon waning immunity after the waning duration

Simple Exposed component for an SEIR/SEIRS model - includes incubation period.

Agents transition from Exposed to Infectious when their incubation timer (etimer) expires. Tracks number of agents becoming infectious each tick in model.nodes.symptomatic.

docs.title: laser.generic.models.SEIRS.Infectious(model, infdurdist, wandurdist, infdurmin=1, wandurmin=1)
docs.text:
Infectious component for an SIRS/SEIRS model - includes infectious duration and waning immunity.

Agents transition from Infectious to Recovered after the infectious period (itimer). Set the waning immunity timer (rtimer) upon recovery. Tracks number of agents recovering each tick in model.nodes.recovered.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility
docs.text: This module defines the Susceptibility class for managing and visualizing the susceptibility of a population in LASER models.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility.Susceptibility(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER model component representing the susceptibility of a population.

This component adds a scalar property susceptibility to the population and a vector property to the patches. It tracks which agents are susceptible at any given tick and provides helper functions for updating these values.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Initialize the susceptibility component of the model.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object A LASER model instance. Must have population, patches, and params with the following: * population.add_scalar_property(name, dtype, default) * patches.add_vector_property(name, nticks, dtype) * params.nticks (int) - number of simulation ticks. verbose : bool, optional If True, enables verbose output. Default is False.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This method adds a scalar property susceptibility to each agent in the population, initialized to 1 (susceptible).
  • It also adds a vector property susceptibility to each patch, which tracks aggregated susceptible counts over time.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility.Susceptibility.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text: Make the component callable. Required for LASER component interface.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object The LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: This component does not perform per-tick updates. The method exists only to satisfy the LASER component interface.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility.Susceptibility.census(model, tick)
docs.text: Aggregate susceptible counts into patches at the given tick.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing population and patches. tick : int Current simulation tick.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At tick 0, this computes susceptible counts from the population and writes them into patches.susceptibility.
  • For a single patch, counts are summed globally.
  • For multiple patches, counts are distributed by agent nodeid.
  • In all cases, values are copied into patches.susceptibility_test for validation/debugging.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility.Susceptibility.on_birth(model, _tick, istart, iend)
docs.text: Handle the birth event by setting susceptibility of newborns.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: model : object LASER model containing the population. _tick : int Current simulation tick (unused). istart : int or ndarray of int64 Start index of newborns (if slice mode), or array of agent indices (if random-access mode). iend : int or None End index of newborns (exclusive). If None, istart is treated as an index array for random-access updates.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: None

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Newborns are set to susceptible (1).
  • Uses either slice-based or random-access update functions.

docs.title: laser.generic.susceptibility.Susceptibility.plot(fig=None)
docs.text: Placeholder for plotting susceptibility distribution by age.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: fig : Figure, optional A Matplotlib Figure to draw into. If None, a new figure would be created in a full implementation.

docs.title: Yields
docs.text: None The generator currently yields once (None). This is a placeholder and should be replaced with actual plotting logic in the future.

docs.title: laser.generic.transmission
docs.text: This module defines the Transmission class, which models the transmission of measles in a population.

docs.title: laser.generic.transmission.Transmission(model, verbose=False)
docs.text:
A LASER-generic component that models inter-agent disease transmission within and between patches.

This component calculates the force of infection at each time step using: - Agent state (infectious status) - Node-level infectious prevalence - Network-based movement between patches - Time-varying transmission modifiers (e.g., seasonality or biweekly scaling)

It supports multiple transmission dynamics depending on the structure of the population (e.g., SI, SEIR).

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Initialize the Transmission component and register per-patch properties.

Adds the following properties: - cases: Total cumulative infections per patch per time step - forces: Force of infection for each patch (float32) - incidence: New infections per patch per time step - doi: Date of infection for each individual (set on infection)

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

A LASER Model instance, which must expose .patches, .population, and .params.

required verbose bool

If True, prints debug info during initialization (currently unused).

False

docs.title: laser.generic.transmission.Transmission.__call__(model, tick)
docs.text:
Compute and apply transmission dynamics for the current tick.

This function: - Computes node-level contagion (infectious density) - Modifies contagion using a network matrix if present - Applies time-varying scalars (seasonality, biweekly modifiers) to beta - Calculates force of infection per patch - Samples new infections using Numba-accelerated kernels depending on model structure

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Notes

The appropriate infection update kernel is selected based on the presence of etimer or itimer.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

The LASER model instance.

required tick int

Current simulation tick.

required

docs.title: laser.generic.transmission.Transmission.census(model, tick)
docs.text:
Snapshot infectious counts into cases_test at tick t and propagate to tick t+1.

This method: - Initializes cases_test[t] using individual infection status - Copies cases_test[t] into cases_test[t+1] to maintain continuity

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Any

The LASER model instance.

required tick int

Current simulation tick.

required

docs.text:
Initialize doi (date of infection) for newborns.

This birth handler sets the date-of-infection field to zero for all new individuals.

The LASER model.

required _tick int

The current tick (unused).

required istart int

Start index of new agents.

required iend int

End index of new agents (exclusive). If None, assumes single agent at istart.

required

Generate a 2x2 subplot figure showing cases and incidence over time.

For the two most populous patches, this creates: - Line plot of cases over time - Line plot of incidence over time
